channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Lula embarca para reunião da Celac, que tenta protagonismo em meio aos movimentos de Trump na América Latina e Caribe
item.description: President Lula traveled to Colombia to participate in the Celac summit, aiming to strengthen Latin America and the Caribbean's autonomy from U.S. pressure, particularly after Trump labeled Brazilian criminal groups as terrorist threats. The summit will reaffirm the region as a zone of peace, combat organized crime through regional police cooperation and intelligence sharing, and advance South-South cooperation with Africa. Brazil’s strategy is to use Celac as a regional shield and integrative platform against external influence.

item.title: Datafolha: Desânimo, insegurança e medo do futuro dominam humor pré-eleitoral
item.description: A majority of Brazilians express insecurity, discouragement, and fear about the future when thinking about the country, according to a Datafolha poll conducted seven months ahead of the 2026 elections. The prevailing negative sentiment reflects concerns about the current situation and pessimistic expectations for the nation's future.

item.title: Ala pró-Lula do Cidadania acusa opositores do governo na legenda de métodos antidemocráticos
item.description: Comte Bittencourt, representative of the pro-Lula faction within the Cidadania party, accused the opposing current linked to former party president Roberto Freire of using antidemocratic methods in the struggle for control of the party. He stated he would not accept such practices, escalating internal conflict within the party. The statement was made on March 20, 2026, reflecting growing tensions over the party's political alignment.

item.title: Em modo campanha, Lula prega contra Bolsonaro em Minas
item.description: President Lula (PT) visited the Iveco factory in Sete Lagoas, Minas Gerais, alongside Senator Rodrigo Pacheco (PSD-MG), in a campaign-style tone. He urged voters to choose their representatives carefully and avoid 'taking the risk' of electing 'a person' who, according to him, poses a danger to the country — an indirect reference to Jair Bolsonaro. The speech included criticisms of the former president and an appeal to defend democracy and national development.

item.title: Lula: Está chegando o momento de a gente começar a saber quem faz ou não faz, quem mente ou não
item.description: President Lula stated that the time is coming for Brazilian society to discern who tells the truth and who does not keep promises, referring to statements made via mobile phones. He emphasized the importance of distinguishing between those who deliver results and those who merely engage in rhetoric.

item.title: Em Minas, Lula adota tom de campanha e faz referências a Bolsonaro
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva held an event in Sete Lagoas, Minas Gerais, highlighting federal government actions in education and warning about the upcoming 2026 elections, indirectly criticizing Jair Bolsonaro’s rhetoric on vaccines and firearms. Lula stressed the need for responsible voting, citing dangerous misinformation such as claims that vaccines cause homosexuality or turn people into alligators. The event included the symbolic delivery of 324 school buses and a 55% increase in school meal funding announced by the Education Minister.

item.title: Flávio anuncia pré-candidatura de Anderson Ferreira ao Senado por PE
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) announced the pre-candidacy of former federal deputy Anderson Ferreira (PL) for the Senate in Pernambuco, emphasizing the need to build a competitive ticket focused on combating violence and crime. The move aims to strengthen the party’s presence in the state and offer voters a distinct political alternative.

item.title: Governo de SP cobre verba federal bloqueada no túnel Santos-Guarujá
item.description: The São Paulo state government issued a decree allocating R$ 2.639 billion in supplementary credit for the Santos-Guarujá immersed tunnel, matching the federal funds blocked by the TCU two days prior. Signed by Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos), the measure aims to supplement the state budget to ensure the project's continuation.

item.title: Guerra pelo TCU só tem homens; corte precisa de uma ministra qualificada
item.description: The dispute over the vacancy left by Minister Aroldo Cedraz at the TCU is marked by fights, accusations of betrayal, and electoral interests in the Chamber of Deputies. All candidates under consideration are men, sparking criticism over the lack of female representation. There is growing pressure for the court to appoint a qualified female minister to balance the tribunal’s composition.

item.title: Governo de SP autoriza R$ 609 mi em bonificação para policiais
item.description: The São Paulo government authorized R$ 609 million in performance-based bonuses for civil and military police for the 2025 fiscal year. Governor Tarcísio de Freitas stated the payment recognizes officers' daily risk and dedication to public safety. The police union Sindpesp acknowledged that the governor is fulfilling part of his 2022 campaign promises by submitting a bill for a new career progression model.

item.title: “Bordando a Democracia”: último episódio debate a participação de mulheres negras e indígenas nas eleições
item.description: The final episode of the podcast 'Bordando a Democracia', produced by the TSE's Secretariat of Communication and Multimedia, discusses the participation of Black and Indigenous women in Brazilian elections, noting that although over half of the 2024 candidacies were from self-declared Black candidates, fewer than 5,000 Black women were elected, revealing a significant gap between candidacies and electoral success. The episode features testimonies from women who participated in TSE events between 2024 and 2025 and includes remarks from TSE President Minister Cármen Lúcia on the historical inequalities faced by Black women in economically and socially vulnerable conditions.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula vai ao Complexo Industrial da Iveco, em Minas
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva visits the Iveco factory in Sete Lagoas (MG) to participate in the delivery of 324 new school buses under the Caminho da Escola program, with 158 units being effectively delivered during the event. This marks the start of the distribution of 1,000 vehicles from the 2nd phase of the Novo PAC Seleções, involving an investment of approximately R$ 500 million, aimed at improving access to basic education in rural, riverine, and hard-to-reach areas. The event includes the presence of Education Minister Camilo Santana and mayors from various regions across the country.

item.title: TSE não decidiu que reduzir combustível em ano eleitoral é crime
item.description: The TSE did not rule that reducing fuel prices in an election year is a crime. Viral social media claims misuse a 2022 CNN Brazil report taken out of context, which discussed an archived consultation without substantive analysis, not a court decision. The Lula government announced measures to lower diesel prices, including zeroing PIS and Cofins and providing subsidies to producers and transporters.

item.title: Papo Antagonista – Desconfiança a ministros do STF atinge recorde histórico
item.description: A poll revealed that distrust in Brazil's Supreme Federal Court (STF) ministers has reached a historic high, reflecting growing public dissatisfaction with the Judiciary. The show also discussed political shifts in Lula's government, including Geraldo Alckmin's potential move to the Senate, and Trump's remarks about labeling PCC and CV as terrorist groups. An interview with Rubinho Nunes covered these topics.

item.title: Ratinho chama Erika Hilton de “malcriada” e diz não temer processo
item.description: TV host Ratinho called deputy Erika Hilton a "spoiled brat" and downplayed the lawsuit filed against him for alleged transphobia, stating he does not believe in a conviction and will not retract his comments about her election to lead the Women’s Rights Defense Committee. He argued that cisgender women are more suited to discuss female-related issues, denied prejudice, and claimed to respect different lifestyles, despite the Federal Prosecutor’s Office and Ministry of Communications reviewing the case. The Federal Police have opened an investigation, SBT issued an apology, while Hilton has government backing and faces opposition in Congress over her leadership.

item.title: Tabata Amaral aciona STF por emendas ligadas a filme sobre Bolsonaro
item.description: Federal Deputy Tabata Amaral (PSB-SP) has requested that Supreme Court Justice Flávio Dino order the TCU and CGU to conduct a special and thorough audit of parliamentary amendments allocated to four companies, including the Instituto Conhecer Brasil, which are linked to a film about former President Jair Bolsonaro. Amaral suspects potential misuse of public funds for political or ideological purposes.

item.title: PE: Flávio Bolsonaro lança Anderson Ferreira como pré-candidato ao Senado. Veja vídeo
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) announced former federal deputy Anderson Ferreira (PL) as the pre-candidate for the Senate in Pernambuco, emphasizing the need to combat violence and high taxes in the state. Ferreira, who was mayor of Jaboatão dos Guararapes and ran for governor of Pernambuco in 2022, stated he has the support of Jair Bolsonaro and that the 'troop is united' for the race.

item.title: Daniel Vorcaro, do Master, dá mais um passo rumo a possível delação
item.description: Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, signed a confidentiality agreement with the Federal Police and is negotiating a plea deal, after changing lawyers and being transferred to the PF superintendency in Brasília under authorization from Minister André Mendonça. Dario Durigan was officially appointed as the new Minister of Finance, replacing Fernando Haddad, who will run for governor of São Paulo as a PT candidate. U.S. President Donald Trump criticized NATO countries for not helping to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, calling them 'cowards' in a social media post.

item.title: A queda de confiança no Supremo
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court is experiencing a decline in public trust, particularly among conservative voters angered by its rulings on anti-democratic acts, as well as former supporters dissatisfied with some justices' conduct unbecoming of their office. The Banco Master scandal has publicly revealed long-standing practices eroding the Court’s legitimacy. Rebuilding this trust is crucial for the stability of Brazil’s constitutional democracy.

item.title: Congresso é instituição com menor índice de confiança entre a população, diz pesquisa
item.description: The National Congress is Brazil's least trusted institution, with 86% of the population saying they 'do not trust' federal deputies and senators, according to an AtlasIntel poll. Distrust also extends to the Supreme Federal Court (STF), where 59.5% of respondents believe ministers lack competence and impartiality, worsened by the Banco Master scandal involving figures from all three branches, perceived as influencing STF rulings by 89.9% of Brazilians.

item.title: O que se sabe sobre polêmica no PSOL relacionada à possível saída de Boulos
item.description: A faction aligned with Guilherme Boulos within PSOL is discussing a potential exit from the party to join the PT, following the rejection of a proposed federation by PSOL leadership. The group views the refusal as a sectarian path of isolation, while Boulos denies any final decision and accuses internal factions of spreading a forged letter to create a false narrative.

item.title: Silveira diz que 53 distribuidoras de combustíveis foram multadas por "preço abusivo"
item.description: Minister of Mines and Energy Alexandre Silveira announced that over 53 fuel distributors were fined in three days for abusive practices during a nationwide task force that inspected 1,192 gas stations. The operation, involving ANP, CADE, PF, MJ and Procons, aims to curb unjustified price hikes labeled as 'criminal speculation'. Despite the fines, diesel prices rose 6.76% in a week to R$ 7.26.

item.title: Procurador pede identificação de ‘vazador’ de inquérito sobre vice do Maranhão ligado a Lula e Dino
item.description: The Attorney General of Maranhão, Danilo de Castro, has requested the State Court of Justice to investigate the alleged leak of a sealed inquiry implicating Vice Governor Felipe Camarão (PT) in a money laundering scheme via Pix and the use of 'laranjas' to conceal R$6.3 million in transfers and R$4.7 million in luxury properties. Camarão, a close ally of President Lula and Supreme Court Minister Flávio Dino, is the PT's pre-candidate for governor and denounced the leak as politically motivated and illegal, while the MP argues the disclosure undermines the integrity of an investigation ongoing for over a year.

item.title: Fux pede destaque, e STF vai julgar privatização da Sabesp em sessão presencial
item.description: Minister Luiz Fux requested a spotlight in the judgment of actions challenging Sabesp's privatization, suspending the virtual analysis and rescheduling it for an in-person plenary session at the STF, with an undefined date. So far, only rapporteur Cristiano Zanin has voted, advocating for the dismissal of the actions due to lack of standing, arguing the claims are generic and should be heard in São Paulo's State Tribunal (TJSP), as they involve factual proofs incompatible with the ADPF. Fux and Zanin met with Governor Tarcísio de Freitas and other ministers to discuss the case.

item.title: Paes faz discurso eleitoral em renúncia e 'jingle' gera saia justa com TRE
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Mayor Eduardo Paes (PSD) delivered an electoral-themed speech during the city’s handover ceremony, effectively launching his pre-campaign for state governor in 2026. Following his remarks, singers Dudu Nobre and Seu Jorge performed a modified samba praising Paes as a future governor, creating an awkward moment with TRE-RJ President Claudio de Mello Tavares, who deemed it illegal early electioneering. Paes deflected blame to the singer, calling it improvisation, while the event also touched on public security and concerns about criminal infiltration in politics.

item.title: Há espaço para uma alternativa a Lula e Flávio, diz Aécio Neves
item.description: Aécio Neves, president of the PSDB, met with Michel Temer (MDB) in São Paulo to discuss forming a 'democratic center' alternative for the 2026 elections, amid polarization between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro. Both stated the political landscape remains undefined and there is room for dialogue and political construction. Talks will continue in the coming weeks, including Baleia Rossi (MDB-SP), aiming to strengthen PSDB-MDB rapprochement.

item.title: Vídeo mostra motoboys durante comemoração, não em protesto contra Lula
item.description: A video showing motorcyclists gathered in celebration of a tribute by councilman Ricardo Teixeira in February 2026 is being miscontextualized on social media as a protest against the Lula government. The Cargo Clube, the group involved in the event, denied any political motive or connection to app-based labor regulation, stating the video is being used to spread fake news. The misinformation circulates amid debate over Bill 152/2025, which aims to regulate motorcycle couriers’ work in passenger transport and app-based deliveries.

item.title: Moraes pede para PGR se manifestar sobre domiciliar a Bolsonaro
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes has requested the Attorney General's Office to provide an opinion on whether former President Jair Bolsonaro should serve his sentence under house arrest.

item.title: Paes deixa prefeitura em meio a articulações para eleição indireta ao governo
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Mayor Eduardo Paes resigned from office to run in the indirect election for state governor at the Alerj, following the departure of Governor Cláudio Castro. He frames his candidacy as a 'civic duty' in response to an institutional crisis, positioning himself against his main rival, Douglas Ruas (PL), an ally of the governor. Paes aims to win over the more conservative interior electorate, despite Lula’s support potentially being a liability in a state that is a stronghold of Bolsonarism.

item.title: Candidatura do governador de Rondônia pode atrapalhar os planos do PL
item.description: The vice-governor of Rondônia, Sérgio Gonçalves (União), after previous conflicts with Governor Marcos Rocha (PSD), signaled a rapprochement, indicating support for Rocha's Senate candidacy. This alignment could weaken the PL's plans in the state, as Rocha is considered the strongest pre-candidate according to polls.

item.title: Lula defende criação de reserva estratégica de combustíveis
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva advocated for the creation of a strategic fuel reserve to stabilize prices and ensure supply amid international instability, citing tensions in the Middle East and Brazil’s 30% reliance on imported diesel. He argued that, despite high costs, such reserves are vital for energy sovereignty, drawing a parallel to the country’s foreign exchange reserves that have shielded Brazil since 2005. Lula also pledged investments in refineries and a strategic production and stock plan, noting that the U.S. and China already maintain similar stockpiles.

item.title: Carlos divulga filme sobre Bolsonaro com estreia em maio
item.description: Carlos Bolsonaro shared clips from the film 'A Colisão dos Destinos', which portrays the life of his father, Jair Bolsonaro, with a premiere scheduled for May 14, 2026. The film includes testimonies from Flávio and Eduardo Bolsonaro, along with footage of Jair Bolsonaro's childhood, political career, 2018 campaign, and the assassination attempt against him.

item.title: Durigan assume a Fazenda e diz continuar corte de benefícios fiscais
item.description: Finance Minister Dario Durigan assumed office on March 20, 2026, stating he will continue the fiscal adjustment initiated by Fernando Haddad, with the priority of linking macroeconomic results to the lives of Brazilian families. He emphasized the linear cut of tax benefits (except constitutional ones), approved by Congress in 2025, and the focus on boosting productivity, improving credit, attracting technology and sustainability investments — including the Eco Invest program and sustainable bonds abroad.

item.title: Boulos reage após grupo anunciar sua saída do PSOL e afirma que está discutindo rumos políticos
item.description: Guilherme Boulos responded to a claim by a dissident faction of Revolução Solidária that he was leaving the PSOL for the PT, calling the note apocryphal and accusing the group of opportunism and desperation. He stated that the movement is internally discussing its political future and regretted that a section of PSOL had 'shrunk' by issuing the false letter. Boulos’ allies deny he is leaving the party, saying the claim is a lie spread by a PSOL faction.

item.title: Impeachment de ministro do STF por impopularidade traz risco à democracia
item.description: The Brazilian Supreme Federal Court (STF) faces growing threats of impeachment against its ministers, particularly Alexandre de Moraes and Dias Toffoli, driven by political motivations from the right, especially after Jair Bolsonaro’s rise to power. While the Banco Master scandal is cited as the official justification, attacks on the STF have persisted for years due to its countermajoritarian rulings—such as legalizing same-sex unions and expanding Indigenous land rights—that challenge conservative political interests. The AtlasIntel/Estadão poll shows 60% of Brazilians distrust the court, a sentiment likely to be exploited in the 2026 elections, potentially leading to impeachment proceedings in 2027 and endangering judicial independence and Brazilian democracy.

item.title: Zanin nega pedido para suspender privatização da Sabesp
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin voted to deny a request to suspend the privatization of Sabesp, stating the court cannot assess alleged irregularities in the concession bid as it would require interpretation of infraconstitutional norms. He noted that the parties already used proper legal avenues, including a rejected action in TJSP in 2022. Minister Luiz Fux requested a spotlight, moving the case to the Court's physical plenary.

item.title: A ‘birra’ que fez Flávio Bolsonaro apoiar Sergio Moro na disputa do Paraná
item.description: The PL decided to support Sergio Moro in the Paraná gubernatorial race after Governor Ratinho Jr. refused to be Flávio Bolsonaro’s running mate in the presidential ticket. Allies of Ratinho Jr. viewed the move as a retaliatory 'birra' due to perceived lack of dialogue, especially since he plans to run for president under the PSD. Flávio Bolsonaro justified backing Moro by citing the PSD’s intention to nominate Ratinho, stating the PL must act accordingly to that positioning.

item.title: ‘Continuamos com a agenda de consolidação fiscal com justiça social’, diz novo ministro
item.description: Brazil's new Finance Minister, Dario Durigan, pledged to continue the fiscal consolidation agenda with social justice initiated by former minister Fernando Haddad, emphasizing ongoing efforts to control fuel prices, reduce tax exemptions, and draft a stronger budget for 2027. He stressed his commitment to national sovereignty and the welfare of the Brazilian people, building on the 2% of GDP fiscal adjustment already achieved. Haddad, alongside President Lula and Durigan, confirmed the transition and is expected to run for governor of São Paulo as the PT's candidate.

item.title: Pode demorar, mas vamos recomprar refinaria na Bahia, diz Lula
item.description: President Lula stated that Petrobras may repurchase the Mataripe refinery in Bahia, formerly known as Landulpho Alves, which was sold to Acelen during the Bolsonaro administration. He emphasized that although the process may take time, the goal is to regain control of the refinery to ensure fair fuel prices. Lula also criticized the sale of BR Distribuidora, arguing that its loss weakens Petrobras’ ability to regulate final consumer prices.

item.title: TSE confirma multa de R$ 15 mil a Pablo Marçal por chamar Nunes de 'canalha'
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) unanimously upheld the conviction of Pablo Marçal for abusive electoral propaganda after he called São Paulo Mayor Ricardo Nunes a 'scoundrel' and 'coward' in a video. The R$15,000 fine was confirmed, rejecting Marçal's appeal.

item.title: Sônia Guajajara deixará ministério para disputar reeleição como deputada federal por SP
item.description: Minister of Indigenous Peoples Sônia Guajajara will leave office on March 30 to run for re-election as a federal deputy from São Paulo. She highlighted her administration’s legacy, including the resumption of indigenous land demarcation and bringing Indigenous issues to the center of public debate. Eloy Terena, the ministry’s executive secretary, is a possible successor.

item.title: O primeiro evento político depois do fim do prazo para desincompatibilização
item.description: The 68th National Congress of Municipalities will take place in São Paulo from April 6 to 8, marking the first major political event after the deadline for incompatibilization ahead of the 2026 elections. The gathering will bring together presidential pre-candidates including Lula, Flávio Bolsonaro, Ronaldo Caiado, Eduardo Leite, Ratinho Júnior, and Romeu Zema, under the theme 'The Country Begins in the Municipality,' serving as a strategic platform for direct dialogue with São Paulo mayors.

item.title: Erika Hilton defende debate sobre fim da escala 6×1 em 2026: “Qual o problema?”
item.description: Federal deputy Erika Hilton (Psol-SP) advocated for discussing the end of the 6×1 work schedule in 2026, rebutting a statement by Fiesp president Paulo Skaf, who claimed election years are not the time to debate reducing the workweek. Hilton challenged the stance, asking 'What's the problem?' and insisting the issue should be on the agenda even during electoral years.

item.title: Flávio diz que apoiou Moro ao governo do PR após saber que Ratinho Jr. será seu adversário
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) announced his support for Sérgio Moro's (União-PR) pre-candidacy for governor of Paraná after learning that current governor Ratinho Jr. (PSD-PR) would be Moro's opponent. He stated the decision was based on the need to unite forces against the current state government.

item.title: De saída da prefeitura, Paes critica Castro e Witzel e agradece a Lula
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Mayor Eduardo Paes handed over office to his deputy Eduardo Cavaliere and announced his pre-candidacy for state governor. During the ceremony, he criticized current governor Cláudio Castro and former governor Wilson Witzel, while thanking President Lula for his support. Paes positioned himself as the leader of the opposition to the current state government, reinforcing his alliance with the national PT.

item.title: O impacto das investigações do caso Master na política da Bahia
item.description: Investigations into the Master bank corruption scandal are impacting politicians across the ideological spectrum in Bahia, including ACM Neto (União-BA), who allegedly received R$ 3.6 million from the bank and asset manager Reag between 2023 and 2024, and Jaques Wagner (PT-BA), linked to his daughter-in-law’s company, BK Financeira, which allegedly received R$ 11 million from the same bank. Both deny wrongdoing, stating the funds were declared and related to legitimate consulting services, while the case is already being weaponized in the October state elections, with Neto running for governor against Jerônimo Rodrigues (PT) and Wagner seeking a Senate seat on the governing coalition’s ticket.

item.title: TSE mantém condenação de Marçal por ofensas a Nunes
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) unanimously upheld the conviction of influencer and former candidate Pablo Marçal (União) for abusive electoral propaganda. The decision confirmed a R$ 15,000 fine imposed for offensive statements made against São Paulo Mayor Ricardo Nunes (MDB) during the 2024 municipal campaign.

item.title: Trump admitiu a conselheiros que deportação em massa foi ‘longe demais’, diz jornal
item.description: Donald Trump told advisors that his mass deportation policy went 'too far' and is considering a shift to focus on arresting immigrants with criminal records, following negative voter reactions and violent ICE operations. The change is being led by White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles and border czar Tom Homan, with a temporary halt on large-scale ICE operations in Democratic-led cities such as Minneapolis, Washington, and Chicago.

item.title: Pesquisa negativa mostra que Gilmar Mendes tem razão ao chorar em Plenário
item.description: The AtlasIntel/Estado survey shows 60% of Brazilians distrust the Federal Supreme Court, up 9 points since August last year, driven by the Master case, which fueled perceptions of external influence and direct ministerial involvement. Despite low overall trust, Justice André Mendonça, rapporteur of the Master case, is the most popular, while Flávio Dino garnered 72% support for opposing judicial perks. Alexandre de Moraes retains 37% approval, reflecting partisan divides, while institutions like the Federal Police and the Army are more admired than the Judiciary.

item.title: PF lidera confiança entre os brasileiros e STF aparece entre os piores avaliados, mostra pesquisa
item.description: The Federal Police leads in public trust among Brazilians at 56%, the highest among 13 institutions surveyed, while the Supreme Federal Court registers 35% trust and 59% distrust. Congress shows the worst performance with only 9% trust and 86% distrust. The poll, conducted March 16–19, 2026 with 2,090 respondents, highlights tensions between the PF and Justice Dias Toffoli of the STF in the Banco Master investigation.
